programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: The Mummy's Tomb
programme.desc: A high priest travels to America with a living mummy to kill those who had desecrated the tomb of an Egyptian princess thirty years earlier.

programme.title: The People Under the Stairs
programme.desc: When Fool breaks into the home of his family's landlords, he discovers that they have mutilated several boys and kept them imprisoned under stairs. As Fool attempts to flee, he meets their daughter, Alice. Can Fool and Alice escape?

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: When a downtrodden janitor, Winston Gooze, is exposed to a catastrophic toxic accident, he’s transformed into a new kind of hero: The Toxic Avenger. Now, Toxie must rise from outcast to savior, taking on ruthless corporate overlords and corrupt forces who threaten his son, his friends, and his community.

programme.title: The Wolf Man
programme.desc: Upon his return to his father's estate, aristocrat Larry Talbot meets a beautiful woman, attends a mystical carnival and uncovers a horrifying curse.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Having not dated since her husband died, Rose reluctantly agrees to go on a blind date. This date turns into a serious relationship when she is invited on a cruise which would involve sharing a stateroom.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose is enamored with a hotel guest who looks exactly like Charlie; Blanche is horrified to discover her visiting son Matthew wants to pursue a career as a comedian.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred wants a new color television set and Lamont wants a new car. The problem is that they cannot afford both of them, so Lamont buys the car. However, after Fred discovers Lamont's purchase, he comes up with a scheme that involves him pretending to have amnesia until Lamont will buy him a color television.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith is surprise to discover that her cousin Amelia's ideal marriage is rotten to the core.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie's steals a box of nails from work and finds himself at the center of a household debate on morality.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Out of curiosity, Archie takes a magazine test about his health habits. When he scores the test, he learns he might die at age 57. Rather than take the test for what it is worth (or heed the advice of the accompanying article), Archie fumes about his apparent fate.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Brian & Kathryn vs. Cynthia & Roushaun vs. Leann & Leslie

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Kelly & Brian vs. Jean & Dora vs. Susan & Kristen

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges run a pet hospital and get an important patient, Garcon, a rich ladies poodle. When dognappers posing as reporters steal the poodle, the boys are in a tough spot.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Small town boy made good, producer Larry returns to his home farm town and asks his friends, Joe and sister Tiny, to join his New York nightclub act.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.sub-title: Sing a Song of Six Pants
programme.desc: The stooges are tailors, and are heavily in debt to the Skin & Flint finance company. When the boys read about the big reward for a fugitive robber, they think it could be the answer to their problems. The bank robber conveniently ducks into their shop and leaves a suit with a safe combination. After his girl friend fails to retrieve it, the robber returns with gang and a wild fight ensues. The boys miss out on the reward but wind up with the crook's bankroll and can pay their creditors.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges hope to collect a reward by proving to a museum that cavemen still exist. They return from their expedition with a film purporting to show some stone age stooges defending their women from other cavemen. The museum curators are about to pay they reward, until they overhear the stooges talking about how they faked the film, with themselves playing the cavemen.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are movers for an express company and on a rainy night are sent to move some junk, including a suit of armor, from a spooky old house. The armor is haunted by the ghost of Sir Tom, who has no intention of leaving. The ghost foils the stooges attempts to take the armor, and is about to skewer them with a sword when it's revealed that the stooges were only telling a bedtime story to their "sons" (also played by the stooges.)

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are musicians at the court of King Cole. When they ask the king's permission to marry their sweethearts, the King agrees, but only after Princess Alicia has married Prince Valiant. This news upsets Mergatroyd, an evil magician who plans to marry the Princess himself and rule the Kingdom. Mergatroyd abducts the Princess, and it's up to the stooges to foil his plans and expose his evil doings.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A campaign boss is looking for three delegates to the presidential convention, delegates that are too stupid to discover that his candidate, Hammond Egger, is a crook. Enter the stooges as janitors sent to clean the man's office. After some of their antics, the boy's suitability for the job is apparent and they're hired. The stooges go to the convention, but double cross their boss and vote for another candidate, Abel Lamb Stewer. When the boss and his muscle man come looking for revenge, the boys defeat them in a wild fight.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: In the wilds of Manitoba near an air force base something is killing the locals and drinking their brains. Most of the populace are convinced that radiation leaks from the radar installation's nuclear-power experiments are the cause of the mysterious deaths.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: The Courtship of Eddie, Dan's Father
programme.desc: Dan's visiting father astonishes everyone by announcing his plans to marry Crystal.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. Roseanne throws Crystal a bridal shower and discovers yet another secret. Ed desperately tried to bond with Crystal's son Lonnie. Dan vehemently opposes this wedding, and is furious with Roseanne for participating in it against his wishes. Convinced that his father will ruin Crystal's life, Dan confronts the man in hopes of getting him to call the wedding off.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky runs away from home to live with Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene is embarassed when Roseanne gives Darlene's home economics class a supermarket lesson in real-life family budgeting; D.J. has to deal with a bully.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is mad at Dan for forgetting Valentine's Day, so he winds up full-figure lingerie shopping; and Darlene's upset when a boy she likes asks out Becky instead.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is torn between taking care of her flu-stricken husband and helping Jackie learn lines for her play.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne may lose her job if she and Dan go on their long-awaited trip to see Wayne Newton perform in Las Vegas.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky's boyfriend Mark is certainly not popular around the Conner house when Dan learns he and Becky tampered with his vintage motorcycle, Dan hits the roof, blaming Becky, while Roseanne heads to Mark's house to try and make him break up with her daughter.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr. Grace and Mr. Rumbold try to instill a sense of fire safety in the staff at Grace Brothers, but with the hilarity of a foreign customer, a kiss-of-life medical dummy and Mrs. Slocombe stuck in a fabric tube, it's impossible.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: After Mrs. Slocombe starts dropping hints that it's her birthday, the staff calculate that she is 50 and plan an appropriate party.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
On this Fantastic Friday, Bill and Toony get a visit from a very special guest Cheryl Chase.
Featured cartoons: Fast and Furry-ous (1949) The Scarlet Pumpernickel (1950) Mouse for Sale (1955) In the Pink (1967) Baton Bunny (1959)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: The mistress of a conductor becomes a target for murder after threatening to tell all to her lover's wife.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude feels her life is in danger from an oversexed housepainter, and reacts hysterically.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude heads for the bottle when she sees an old friend in a wheelchair.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's sister, Virginia, comes to Miami for a visit and Blanche is convinced she's after something. Later, while out to dinner, she learns just what Virginia is after, Blanche's kidney. Meanwhile, Rose and Dorothy babysit for an infant.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: A doctor Dorothy dates makes a pass at Blanche. But when Blanche tells Dorothy about it, Dorothy doesn't believe her. Dorothy accuses her of being a slut and when Blanche can't take no more she orders Dorothy to move out.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's 14-year old grandson comes to Miami for a visit and brings a new meaning to the term "generation gap." Meanwhile, Dorothy studies for her French final.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another exciting and action-packed edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring a look at the recent issues between Hulk Hogan and WWE Champion "Macho Man" Randy Savage. Plus, The Rockers are in action, Ted DiBiase looks for a championship belt worthy of "The Million Dollar Man," and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The kids hunt for treasure in an abandoned taffy factory and Bob and Linda must find them before the factory’s scheduled demolition

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Trying to impress Robert when they visit the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, Ray uses his “weight” as a sports writer for Newsday to jump to the front of the line to meet the members of the ’69 Miracle New York Mets.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray confides to Debra that her constant need to cuddle and “be on top of him” when they are sleeping is causing him many sleepless nights, Debra is more than happy to comply with his request to give him his space in bed.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie invite Ray and Debra Barone over for dinner.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie's (Leah Remini) friend Susie (played by Melissa Samuels) and her husband Mike (played by Ben Bode) are over for dinner at the Heffernan's and Carrie invites them to spend Thanksgiving weekend with them. Doug (Kevin James) hates her husband, so he convinces Spence (Patton Oswalt) to move out and stay with them so Carrie's friends can't.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry dates a woman whose boss has a grudge against him; Elaine dates a Communist whose ideology spreads to George and Kramer.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry wants to switch from dating a non-laughing woman to dating her roommate; Elaine has difficulty retrieving a tennis racket she loaned out; George enlists Kramer's mother to spy on his seemingly bulimic girlfriend.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Brutally honest Roseanne suffers the consequences after telling Bev that she's had her fill of her at the Lunch Box. Meanwhile, Jackie jumps back into the dating arena.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie and Roseanne plan a housewarming party for Jackie's new home and try to hide it from their mom. Roseanne invites Darlene home for the weekend to get her back together with David. D.J. goes through a rebellious period of skipping school and enlisting Darlene's help in covering for him, and Roseanne is determined to find out what's going on. Fred pursues Jackie after their one-night stand.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: The Dunder Mifflin Infinity website is launching and Michael is excited about going to the big launch party in New York while Angela plans a satellite party for the Scranton branch. Meanwhile, Dwight competes against the website to see who can sell the most paper in one day.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: As Jan renovates the condo, Michael confronts his growing debt every way he can, which includes getting a second job. Pam and Jim spend a night out on Dwight's family beet farm, now a bed and breakfast.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Grampa needs a kidney transplant Homer is the obvious donor but he chickens out and runs away to sea. On board ship his fellow passengers are disgusted by his cowardice and throw him overboard. Drifting to the shore he sees a father and son bond over building a sand-castle and knows what he must do. The transplant is a success - though its circumstances are unusual,to say the least.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a riot at a sci-fi convention, Mayor Quimby hires Homer as his bodyguard, and Homer witnesses the rampant corruption in town politics. When Homer takes a stand, he runs afoul of the Springfield mafia.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is astonished to learn that Ned is sixty years old and,unusually, Ned admits that he wished he had done some more reckless things in his life so Homer takes him to Las Vegas. Here they both get extremely drunk and wake up to find that they have married cocktail waitresses Amber and Ginger. Eventually the two men are thrown out of Las Vegas and hitch a ride back to Springfield,Homer getting attacked by a vulture.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After drunks vandalize the school the children are blamed and subject to a curfew. Having seen a creepy 'Village of the Damned'-type movie 'The Bloodening' Bart leads the kids in a revolt against their elders,frightening them into believing the children have supernatural powers. But then the senior citizens arrive and they reckon that everybody under the age of 70 should be made subject to a curfew.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and his friends go to the Super Bowl but when their tickets turn out to be fake they get thrown into jail. Fortunately they are freed by Dolly Parton and make their way to a director's box but this is owned by the 'billionaire tyrant' Rupert Murdoch, who orders the visitors' arrested. After being chased onto the field ,Homer and his friends cause considerable confusion which ends with Homer in possession of the Vince Lombardi trophy.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al has finally saved up a lot of cash to buy his family a ton of presents they want this year. Unfortunately, a mob of late customers in the shoe store prevents him from getting to the bank and retrieving the money he needs.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al gets a visit from a guardian angel and discovers what the Bundy household would look like if he was never born.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al desperately tries to fix the house's leaking roof during a rainstorm. But each time Al falls off the roof, it makes Peggy and the kids wonder if he's too stupid or too cheap to call a roofer to do the job. Meanwhile, Steve lands another job, at a pet store, and Marcy suffers side effects after being bitten by a venomous "Peruvian Devil Gerbil."

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After Peggy lied on the Bundy taxes, Al has to find a quick way to raise money for the audit, and when a couple offers good money for Peggy's hair, Al must find a way to convince her.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When Al challenges the selfish and ungrateful family members to earn a dollar of their own, Peggy tries scamming money from others because of her loathe for work. Meanwhile, Bud appoints himself as Kelly's agent and lands her a job as a 'rock video slut.'

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: You Gotta Know When to Fold 'Em (1)
programme.desc: Steve is history after he okays a divorce from Marcy and leaves her in the middle of the night, leaving behind a note saying that he has gone to become a park ranger at Yosemite National Park. While Bud unsuccessfully tries to score with man-less Marcy, Peggy talks her into a "just girls" vacation to Las Vegas, where they lose $5,000 of Al's money.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: You Gotta Know When to Fold 'Em (2)
programme.desc: Al and the kids travel to Las Vegas to try to get the ladies out of the hole they are in. After an unsuccessful attempt to win their money back at the roulette table, Al agrees to try to win $10,000 by staying three minutes in the ring with GLOW wrestler "Big Bad Mama", a huge monster of a woman.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's friend Herman has just gotten out of prison and doesn't have a place to stay. So Lamont invites him to stay at the Sanford residence. However, when Grady learns that he's an ex-con, he nails down everything in the house. Meanwhile, Lamont finds Herman a job at Esther's hardware store and doesn't tell her that he has served time.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont comes home one evening and discovers Fred preparing a gourmet dinner and learns it's to celebrate the arrival of an old friend of Fred's, Bowlegs, a musician, who's visiting. Bowlegs' act is to be put on at a nightclub but when his partner strains his back, Fred and Lamont must fill in to fulfill the nightclub's requirements.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont's politically involved girlfriend stresses involvement in the community's problems. This leads Lamont to phone into a radio talk show to express his views. Positive reaction leads to Lamont deciding to run for state assembly. But Fred soon finds out that this means that Lamont may move to Sacramento and leave the junk business.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The cast appears on Wheel and Deal, hosted by Harry Monty.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris and Larry join Mr. Peterson on a camping trip, when Mr. Peterson manages to lose them, the hungry boys eat some berries that cause them to hallucinate.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris becomes a construction worker with the men who are renovating his parents kitchen. When they tell him one of their trade secrets, Chris has a problem with their dishonesty.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck wreaks havoc on a movie set at Wonder Pictures.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Egghead inspires to become a boxer.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Two curious dogs enter a modern house and end up in hijinks.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and his pal Dizzy Duck go fishing.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: It's amateur night at the local theatre.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky works at the Snappy Rubber Company.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag cartoon of the different animals at the zoo.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Dog prisoners try to escape from prison.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Todd needs a place to stay while being pursued by a rival gang, so he chooses Butt-head's house and directs the boys to 'act dumb' and keep him safe.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
While taking change from a fountain at the mall the boys must run to a fake island in the center when the water is turned on,they remain and think of ways to get out without going into the water...which is only about a foot deep.
videos:
Whale - "Hobo Humpin' Slobo Babe"
The Cure - "Caterpillar"
Beck - "Pay No Mind"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Van Driessen sends the boys on a petition drive to stop a furrier from coming to town.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butthead - King Turd Collection - https://archive.org/details/beavis-and-butthead-king-turd-collection

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: They're Coming To Take Me Away, Huh Huh
programme.desc:
Highland High's new school psychiatrist performs a battery of tests on the boys -- including the Rorschach test (in which they can only see one "thing").
videos:
Smashing Pumpkins - "Today"
Salt 'N Pepa - "Shoop"
Gwar - "Jack The World"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to empty their minds and find they are surprisingly good at it.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
"Beavis and Butter" contribute art to an exhibit of local talent at the mall, and make friends with a sock-puppet.
videos:
Bell Biv DeVoe - "Gangsta"
Death - "The Philosopher"
Vixen - "Edge Of A Broken Heart"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After the new phone book is delivered, the boys decide to call someone, and start repeatedly terrorizing a guy named Harry Sachz with prank calls from the bathroom. Harry gets caller ID and traces the call to Butt-head...who gives him Stewart's address.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head learn how to be movers and break stuff from two guys who rob the Stevensons.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to get rich by breeding dogs with a raccoon they found in a garbage can.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Conservative talk show host Gus Baker (an animated Rush Limbaugh clone) loses his show when he makes the mistake of inviting two "fine upstanding young men as Beavis and Butt-head" to discuss music videos.
videos:
Jawbox - "Savory"
The Clash - "Should I Stay Or Should I Go? (Live)"
Herb Alpert - "North On South St."

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: It's no secret that today's secret word is "fun," 'cause Pee Wee just won a dinner for two at a Hawaiian restaurant! The question is -- who's he gonna take? The whole gang, including Captain Carl and Miss Yvonne, "lei"s on the guilt for poor Pee Wee. Even Cowntess the cow has bought a new Moo-Moo! What's Pee Wee to do? Throw his own wild luau, that's what -- and invite the entire Playhouse!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee tries to entertain himself and his playhouse friends during a rainy day, by playing games, making hot chocolate, telling a ghost story, watching cartoons and even making prank phone calls, but eventually he suffers from "playhouse fever" before wishing away the rainy day.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline show their true colors when the diner gets robbed. Meanwhile, everyone sees a surprising new side of Han.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: On this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host an exciting lineup of in-ring competition, featuring Rick Martel and King Haku meeting one-on-one. Plus, Ted DiBiase finally receives a championship belt worthy of his name, The Red Rooster takes on Mr. Perfect, and more.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Ten years later. Rhaenyra navigates Alicent's continued speculation about her children, while Daemon and Laena weigh an offer in Pentos.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The Night King and his army have arrived at Winterfell and the great battle begins. Arya looks to prove her worth as a fighter.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A 16-year-old Hurricane Katrina victim suffering from hallucinations as a result of the tragedy is brought to House by his former bandmate who recently found out the girl is his daughter. Although House fears his friend is being scammed, he takes the case. As he works his way through the girl's lies so he can diagnose and treat her, he's forced to tell a few lies of his own.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: While trying to figure out the disease of a man whose tongue has swollen grossly, an old patient of Dr. House comes back to seek revenge on the doctor due to the pain he caused his family. House wakes up a patient of his medical team, with one odd anomaly: his leg no longer hurts. After other scientifically improbable and horrific accidents throughout the day, House discovers that what's going on around him isn't real, that he's hallucinating.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer teaches a class on marriage, but sharing secrets about his own union gets him kicked out of the house.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In a Rear Window parody, the Simpsons buy a swimming pool and everybody in the neighborhood joins in the enjoyment--except for Bart, whose broken leg prevents him from swimming. He soon becomes obsessed with looking through his telescope and even suspects Ned Flanders of murdering his wife.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa feels threatened when a new girl moves to Springfield: Allison is smart, pretty, a great saxophone player and a crack diorama builder. Lisa tries to befriend her new rival, but she's consumed with envy and competitiveness.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After reading the weepy best-seller The Bridges of Madison County, Marge sits down with the family and recalls romantic moments, along with temptations from her bowling instructor Jacques and Homer's coworker Mindy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons go to Itchy and Scratchyland,a theme park based on the violent television cartoon, where Bart and Homer are both arrested for annoying actors dressed as Itchy. However when the robot characters turn violent in the manner of 'Westworld' and the staff evacuate,the Simpsons are the heroes of the day by defeating the robots by using camera flash bulbs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Sideshow Bob is released from prison, runs for mayor as a Republican, and defeats "Diamond" Joe Quimby in the election. But a "Deep-Throat"-type informant tells Bart and Lisa that he was fraudulently elected. It's up to them to find the evidence.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons get hired by Mr Burns to take care of his country-side mansion for the winter where Homer starts going mad. In the second segment, Homer's toaster breaks and in trying to fix it, he inadvertently made it into a time-machine in which cause terrible consequences. In the third segment, Bart and Lisa's teachers start killing and eating the students in the school when they misbehave.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart falls for Reverand Lovejoy's beautiful, yet manipulative, daughter Jessica Lovejoy who has a way of making boys do whatever she wants them to do but when but when Bart is accused by the whole town of stealing money from the church collection plate Lisa springs into action to find the real thief and all fingers point to Ms Lovejoy. Meryl Streep guest voices.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa's surprising ability at hockey spawns jealousy in Bart and a showdown when their teams meet.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gropes for a gummy candy stuck to the babysitter's pants, she mistakes his intentions and turns the town against him.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: The New Age Outlaws challenge Kane & Mankind for the Tag Team Championship with special referees Undertaker and WWE Champion "Stone Cold" Steve Austin. The Nation takes violent exception to DX's recent portrayal of them. Plus, Shawn Michaels appears, Val Venis offers an apology to Mr. Yamaguchi-San with a look at his newest video, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny perceives the town's welcome sign to be obscene, but the townspeople don't agree. Meanwhile, Moira falls into a dark depression after googling herself, David seeks employment and Alexis fawns over Mutt.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Alexis does community service with Mutt; Stevie convinces David to sell his clothes; and Moira and Johnny contemplate whether or not they're bad parents after they realize they don't know their children's middle names.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to embrace Bobby's hobby of bowling, but she fears she is his bad luck charm when she accidentally injures his teammate's hand and threatens their chance for a championship. Also, Max uses her bowling prowess to get onto Bobby's team, and Sophie worries that baby Barbara might not be smart enough,

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Rock Me on the Dais
programme.desc: Caroline's ex-boyfriend, Candy Andy, shows up at a press junket being held to promote the film about Caroline's life; Bobby gets jealous over Caroline and Candy Andy's reconnection.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The women and the diner gang prepare for the premiere of the film about Caroline's life; Caroline and Max contemplate their future at the diner.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max invites Caroline to be her roommate, but their new friendship is jeopardised when Max's boyfriend flirts with Caroline.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Thinking she is being helpful, Caroline facilitates Max's breakup with Robbie.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A battle erupts over a shirt after Max introduces Caroline to shopping at thrift stores; a sarcastic street artist flirts with Max.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline asks Max to help her break into her former townhouse to retrieve her much-needed night guard.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max throw a '90's-themed party at the diner to attract rich hipsters and raise money for their cupcake business; Caroline is horrified when her rich ex-boyfriend comes into the diner.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is tired of sleeping on the couch, and purchases a build-it yourself Murphy Bed; Max tries to work up the courage to give her cupcake business card to Peach.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline persuades Max to take a cupcake-decorating class.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The two take a job cleaning a hoarder's apartment for the extra money; Max learns that Johnny has a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Johnny's girlfriend has a devious plan in mind when she hires Max and Caroline to bake cupcakes for an art show featuring his work.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend Caroline's first broke holiday season working at a department store's Santaland to earn extra money.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to find Chestnut a job in order to get him lodging for the winter. Meanwhile, Peach, the mother of the twins Max babysits, auditions for "Real Housewives of TriBeCa".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max's stove breaks, Caroline tries to return some expensive rings to Barney's department store, but discovers her surname is no longer an asset at her former home away from home.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: It's Ted's first day of coaching, and fans aren't happy. He makes little headway but remains undeterred as the team plays its first match.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: To arrange an in-depth exposé, Rebecca pairs cynical journalist Trent Crimm with Ted for a day. Ted and Roy venture into the community.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca hosts the team's annual charity benefit, where Ted stages a reconciliation between Roy and Jamie.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A disparaging remark about his wife sets Johnny Sack off on a vendetta. Meadow decides to do volunteer work at the South Bronx Law Center; Furio hosts a housewarming party for the gang; Silvio and Chris make an offer to an old-time hit man named Lou DiMaggio; and Tony and Carmela find themselves at odds over the family's bill-consolidation strategy.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Checking out Ralph's promising new filly at the track, Tony displays a flair for horse-racing strategy--and discovers an unexpected affinity for the stable life. Adriana finds her rock club being used as a place of business for Chris and his pals. Meanwhile, a court artist incurs the wrath of Uncle Junior, and Janice feeds Bobby in his hour of need.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor makes a surprising personal discovery. Meanwhile, Michael tasks an indecisive Chidi with coming to an important decision and Jianyu makes a big announcement.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor and Tahani hatch a plan while Michael makes a startling discovery. Meanwhile, Chidi confides in Eleanor after learning some interesting news.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As the families gather on Driftmark for a funeral, Viserys calls for an end to infighting and Alicent demands justice.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E12 - The Pact [SHDW, Mark SHaDoW]
programme.desc: Gumball and Principal Brown make a pact to tell each other what problems their girlfriends have.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E13 - The Neighbor [SHDW, Mark SHaDoW]
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin try to find out the name of their neighbor, Antlers Guy, but accidentally blow his witness protection cover.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.sub-title: The Amazing World of Gumball S06E14 - The Shippening [SHDW, Mark SHaDoW]
programme.desc: Sarah finds a notebook from the Awesome Store which magically makes what she draws happen. Unfortunately, she draws bizarre love stories throughout Elmore.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Anais has been facepalming every time her family does something stupid, which has led to a serious medical condition. In fact, if she facepalms just one more time she could do her brain irreparable damage, so the Wattersons have to get through the week without doing anything idiotic.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole and her parents reconnect after a long forced absence.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole decides to pay the cable bill in person at Chanax, she leaves specific instructions for Richard to stay in the car. However he's far too tempted by the vending machine in the foyer to follow her advice. Chanax believes Richard is their mysterious founder and follow his every whim, leading to their inevitable demise.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are at the convenience store considering playing truant. Larry warns them that if they don't take their education seriously they'll end up like him. To prove his point, Larry lets Gumball and Darwin work some of his many roles to make them appreciate the value of a good education.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin argue with the Internet and make him mad. So mad in fact that he seeks to wipe out humanity and, while doing so, malfunctions! The whole of Elmore is plunged into a time before technology. It's actually pretty nice. Will Gumball and Darwin decide to leave the internet unplugged?

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin use a magic potion to shrink Hector.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Rob creates a variety of spinoff Elmore shows and formats to replace Gumball.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin tries to teach the Titans valuable life lessons by reading them fairytales, but the others co-opt his stories and spin them into their own twisted yarns.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The titans are brought to court for the destruction they caused to Jump City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Hive succeed in destroying the Titans, but their ghosts come back to haunt them all.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy's presses the fire button on Cyborg's laser cannon and the Titans die, but Beast Boy puts his blood in them to save them from dying and now have the ability to transform into animals and act like animals.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's Beast Boy's birthday, but no one will celebrate it.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After refusing to shop on Black Friday, Starfire is visited by the ghosts of Black Friday past, present, and future.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans visit the Hall of Justice to use the pool, but then decided to check out the inside.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans must save the members of the Justice League from the clutches of the supervillan Darkseid.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans attempt to infiltrate Santa's workshop to get their names taken off the Naughty List.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: It's another warped day for Commander Höek and Cadet Stimpy as they fly into a mind-bending black hole, mutate continuously, and discover a mountain of missing left socks.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy invents a Happy Helmet so that his foaming friend Ren can be happy all the time. But it pushes Ren way past happiness and into utter insanity - and the nonstop "Happy, Happy, Joy, Joy" song doesn't help either!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy join the Army, where they begin basic training under a very demanding Drill Sargent.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy join the Army, where they begin basic training under a very demanding Drill Sargent. We then follow the unusual and bizarre adventures of "Powdered Toast Man" as he is called on for various rescue missions.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren learns the benefits of dental hygiene the hard way, as he refuses to listen to Stimpy's advice on brushing your teeth. Then Ren and Stimpy are tossed in the pound, where they discover the true meaning of "going to sleep".

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy hit the road as door-to-door Rubber Nipple Salesmen, peddling their wares to a bizarre clientele, including a psychotic Fire Chief, a paranoid Mr. Horse, and a family actually in need of new nipples!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The ""Positive Acting Teens"" group, led by Mr. Graham, seeks to rehabilitate some kids in detention with community service. Fat chance, since those kids happen to be Beavis and Butt-head.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go investigate a crime scene, Beavis gets arrested after nearly killing Butt-head.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys want to sue a chick in class for sexual harassment because she's constantly giving them ""wood.""

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The two moronic metal-heads spike a carton of milk with Spanish fly, hoping a girl they want to have sex with will drink it. Instead a jock on the Highland High wrestling team who's been hassling them takes a swig of it, and he's scheduled to wrestle with Beavis in the next gym class!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: A bus trip to the mountains ends in disaster for Mr. Van Driessen.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Wheel of Fortune Runs Over People and Flattens Them will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast begins to rely too heavily on good luck charms to get them through this episode.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The A-Team Delivers Newspapers will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast takes a look at the current job market for kids.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Algebra is Exciting! will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast has to stay awake during an all-night filming of the show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Falcon Crest Eaten by Termites will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast talks about their homes, and all the stuff they do there.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Top Gun Gets Put on Latrine Cleaning Duty will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross becomes a drill sergeant to induce discipline in the cast.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Greatest Detective will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Some of the cast members, and a plate of Barth Burgers disappear, so the great Inspector Flusseau comes in to investigate.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Reading Rambo will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The Librarian is very happy about this week's episode being about reading, and gets ready for her first big scene. She becomes upset, however, that there are no sketches in this episode that take place in the library.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Huxtables Put Their Kids Up for Adoption will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
This episode features many sketches about adoption and parents.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Crocodile Dundee Works in a City Bank will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross plans to take the cast to New York, but he is planning to keep them on leashes for safety.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Real life and wild life battle it out like animals on this episode of MXC, as reality TV stars face off against animal specialists.
Games include: Window Pain; Dash to Death; Loogie Launch; Endangering Species; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle looms between office workers and toy makers in this MXC battle.
Games include: Holes of Glory; Skidmarkers; Log Drop; Chum in the Mouth

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Can the military take down the big lacquered hair, perfect bodies and personalities of the beauty pageant contestants?
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Turtle Hurdlers Crossing the Poo-Tomac; Pole Riders; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hoteliers go head-to-head with entrepreneurs in this battle. Captain Tenneal speaks on the dangers of hotel rooms.
Games include: Dirty Muddy Balls; Saddle Sores; Staff Infectors; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
In this episode, former Olympians from America battle former Olympians from the rest of the world. Kenny and Vic try to conceal their USA pride. The Captain hangs out with pop sensation Pey'once.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Dope on a Rope; Dash to Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Members of entertainment media take on union workers on this episode of MXC. Vic and Kenny discuss the benefits of union employment.
Games include: Window Pain; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Muddy Runs; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle rages between those who help people get hitched and those who hitch trailers. Also, Vic and Kenny get a visit from some truckers, and Ken practices his future work as a Justice of the Peace.
Games include: Mudd Butlers; Tour de Grand Prix; Brass Balls; Nut Baggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
MXC goes under the lights for the first nighttime edition as Wall Street's Bulls and Bears challenge Bourbon Street's distillers and beer makers. Also, Vic preaches to Kenny about the dangers of alcohol.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Eat Shitake; Pole Riders; Log Drop

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria helps Jane dye her hair with less than stellar results.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High is low on funds and the students and Faculty need to start cutting corners.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn reflects on anniversaries and her many admirers.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The fashion club sets new membership guidelines.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn attend a camp reunion where a greased watermelon lies in wait.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Fashion Club is inspired to do community service.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The teachers of Lawndale High demand their rights.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The members of Mystic Spiral are traumatized by an exploding fog machine.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Bad Luck Eye of the Little Yellow God
programme.desc: Baron Silas Greenback has stolen the Bad Luck Eye of the Little Yellow God from a remote mountain tribe in Brasil and uses it to harness a laser with which to zap innocent bystanders into having bad luck. Dangermouse and Penfold are unlucky enough to recover the jewel, but have great misfortune trying to return it from whence it came.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to get Danger Mouse to bring him four ingredients needed for an evil spell. Danger Mouse meets Count Duckula when one ingredient turns out to be the feather of a vampire duck.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback shrinks himself and enters Colonel K's body to learn all of the Colonel's secrets.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback vows to blow up every single signpost in every country unless he is proclaimed emperor of the world. DM and Penfold set out for the Toad's secret island hideout somewhere in the pacific ocean.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback is determined to steal plans that Danger Mouse and Penfold are transporting from Australia to London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Strange Case of the Ghost Bus
programme.desc: Nine Top Secret cargo carrying ships have disappeared after encountering a ghost bus. DM finds the Ghost bus depot on a small island in the Indian Ocean where Baron Greenback is producing buses made of ice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: After half the world's most famous monuments have vanished, Dangermouse and Penfold head for America, where they find the Tower of London in the old west. It soon becomes apparent that Baron Greenback has invented a machine to shrink, transport and de-shrink objects (and heroes like Dangermouse).

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Electrocuted Hot Dog, Household Levers, Fly's Foot, Chocolate Journey, Watch, Mass vs Weight and more
programme.desc: Electrocuted Hot Dog, Household Levers, Fly's Feet, Magic Number, Oil Lamp, Chocolate Journey, Quartz Watch, Mass vs. Weight. Leila describes the various ways to cook a hot dog: boiling. fry, and baking in an oven. Mr. Wizard says they are going to electrocute one.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals how microwave ovens work and shows a cross-section model of a spider that demonstrates how they spin silk. He als discusses how they can be a crucial part of our ecosystem by eating harmful insects.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the human ear, why we have earwax, and why they pop when they are under pressure. He also introduces Robert Goddard, the father of modern rocketry.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses the ozone layer and why it is rapidly depleting. He also explains the complexities of acids.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses how plumbing works, and why the world is a better place with it. He also explains how and why roller coasters work.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses how bees live and produce honey. He also explains how shifting plates on the earth's surface cause earthquakes.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Don't stay in the dark - Bill Nye will help you absorb the science of light optics. Light is energy that normally moves in a straight line, but often something gets in the way. When light runs into something, three things can happen - the light can bounce off, it can go through, or it can be absorbed. Often all three things happen at the same time. Light bounces off mirrors. You see yourself in a mirror when light bounces off your face, into the mirror, and then into your eyes. Light goes through glass. If the glass is bent or curved, the light gets bent on its way out of the glass. The glass in a magnifying glass or a pair of eyeglasses is curved so that it bends light, making things look bigger. More light is absorbed by dark-colored things than by light-colored things. Colors are made when some light is absorbed while other light is bounced back. Black things look black because when light hits them, they absorb almost all of the light.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: In this show, you can Bone up on Muscles. When you clicked on the Nye Labs web site to read this, you used your bones and muscles. Without them, you can't click, surf, or even sigh. Bones and muscles work together, or you aren't going anywhere. Muscles always pull, even when you push on something like a door somewhere in your body your arm and leg muscles are in tension. They are all attached to bones, and those bones are pushing; they're in compression. By pulling on bones you can breathe, talk, and move all over the world. Your bones support your weight like beams of steel or wood. They're stiff and strong. Rigid as they might seem though, they do flex. And, if you bang one hard enough, it swells up. You have a lump. That's because bones are full of blood vessels. Bones are not solid like rocks or skeletons in a dinosaur museum. Bones flex and grow. In fact, putting healthy amounts of stress on your bones is good for them.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball is asked to settle a dispute between Penny and her family.
